auth_notes: Requires an API Key typically passed in the 'Authorization' header as a Bearer token or via a custom header like 'X-API-KEY'. Some niche providers in this category also use Basic Auth with an API Secret.
rate_limits: Typically 60-120 requests per minute; specific limits are often undocumented and vary by subscription tier.
gotchas: Pagination is often not standardized, requiring custom 'Loop' logic in n8n to fetch all referral records.
Date-time formats for no-show timestamps often require strict ISO 8601 conversion using n8n expressions.
Webhook payloads may lack detailed object data, requiring a follow-up GET request to fetch the full referral or appointment details.
client_must_provide: Documentation for specific JSON schema requirements
research_notes: As this appears to be a niche or vertical-specific API (likely for medical, dental, or appointment-based services), the integration will rely heavily on the n8n HTTP Request node. Developers will need to manually map JSON fields. If the service supports webhooks, a 'Webhook' node can be used to trigger workflows when a no-show is logged or a referral is submitted, but manual registration of the webhook URL via the service's dashboard or a POST request will be necessary.
